description: reads data that was written to the event ID storage of the global routing manager

parameterGroups.label: Slot Configuration
parameterGroups.rationale: These parameters configure which data slot to read and whether the slot index is fixed at compile time.

gaps.question: What is the 'event ID storage of the global routing manager'? Is it a per-event-ID key-value store with 16 slots per event? How is data associated with a specific event ID?
gaps.context: Users need to understand the data model: events carry additional data slots that can be written by event_data_writer and read by event_data_reader.

gaps.question: When does the reader read the data -- on note-on events (handleHiseEvent), on every process() call, or triggered by some other mechanism? What event types trigger a read?
gaps.context: Determines whether this is an event-driven or continuously-polling reader.

gaps.question: The Static parameter (0/1 toggle with converter) -- does it fix the SlotIndex at compile time for C++ export? Or does it change the reading behaviour (e.g., read once vs read continuously)?
gaps.context: The text converter will provide label text, but the behavioural difference needs C++ confirmation.

gaps.question: The node has ModulationTargets (modulation output). What value does it output -- the raw stored data, or a normalised 0..1 version? What is the output range?
gaps.context: Determines how the read value can be used downstream as a modulation source.

gaps.question: With IsPolyphonic true, does each voice read data associated with its own note-on event ID? How is the per-voice event ID resolved?
gaps.context: Critical for understanding polyphonic use cases where different voices read different stored values.

notes: routing.event_data_reader reads from a per-event data storage system managed by the GlobalRoutingManager. It is polyphonic and processes HiseEvents. The companion node is routing.event_data_writer. Together they enable passing arbitrary data between nodes via the event ID system -- for example, storing a random value at note-on and reading it later in the signal chain. The modulation output suggests the read value is forwarded as a modulation signal. 16 data slots are available per event (SlotIndex 0-16).
